Vems?

200m 32amp cable
800m 16amp cable
1x63 amp central (by the main power line in the pyramid)
2x32 amp centrals (in the middle of the long western line)
8x16 amp centrals (spread out over the 16 amp network)

This assumes that The Port can be powered from the buildings up by the gate. We need to check with Michael that we can use power from there, or there needs to be another 200m of 16amp.

The survival guide reads: "Most camping places will be within 200m of a 10A, 220V power outlet. Bring your own extension chords." My suggestion brings the whole quarry within the 200 meter limit, but could probably be rationalized more.
Textilbrudar?
I'll just leave this here.

As you all know, The Borderland is sold out and you will not be able to attend unless you are a registered member. Everyone will be checked in by name at the gate. Please bring ID.
There will, of course, be those who don't respect the integrity of the community and will try to get in anyway. As much as we would like everyone to be able to attend, this is not possible. To disregard this and decide to sneak in is not only a violation of the co-creators who fully engage planning and organizing this event, but also of all those who stayed home.
The responsibility will lie on all of you to help us keep uninvited guests out. If you meet someone without a bracelet, please take help from your friends and escort them out. This event will not be able to live on and grow unless we can find a way to manage this together.

Barn lovers! I'm trying to remember where the doors to the barn were. Marked them in red on a snapshot of @[732982993:2048:Daniel]'s map. Is my memory correct?

The reason I'm asking is that The Borderland Charter Travel Agency is hoping to to camp between the barn and the main road. Just to the right of you guys. However, we want to ensure that there is a good way to get from the road to the barn.
I'm assuming that the leftmost door will be in the middle of rabbitville with no clear pathway to it. So... should the middle door be the main door, with a pathway between our two camps or should we rather try to pimp a great lit up pathway going on the right side of the building up to the blown out wall on the short side of the building?

What are your thoughts?

I'm a very forgiving person and understand that shit happens. I just don't like when people don't take responsibility of their mistakes :/

This will be our third year at Boesdal, but will it also be our last? Moving on to our fifth stop in our collective journey will take some work, as we've become quite a tribe nowadays.
Please join us in #futurelocation on Slack to help scout and negotiate future venues for our event and community to grow! Dare to dream! Dare to explore!

The main lake has tall drops (2m+) on one side, and a more rocky beach on the other side.
The main lake. Those wind mills could power the entire event.
The second largest lake, with an abandoned building.
The sand beach on the ocean is about 400m long. It is straight towards the open sea, so it might be a bit windy some days. But it is worth noting that Gotland is the most weather safe spot in Sweden.
Inside the building in the lake. It is possible to swim in from below.

I created a "Matter Out Of Place", for things found in places where they shouldn't be. If you find unnamed trinkets left in public spaces, this is where you put them. Every now and then we will set a deadline for recovery and throw away what's left.
You all have the mandate to put things in here. Whenever you think "what the fuck is this thing doing here", this is the place it goes :)

Participatory culture, co-creation, Burning Man and the evolution of consciousness. Startups, experiments, mvpâs, innovation and disruption. Self-management, metamodernism, teal, integral. Curiosity, empathy, humility and self-esteem. These are a few of my favorite things. (No, the melody doesnât match, donât even try it).

Hello, lovely people. Thank you for inviting me to this prestigious group of highly functional individuals. I thought Iâd introduce myself in case we run in to each other at some cool Hyper Project of the Future.

Iâve been speaking at a couple of master classes back in 2010-2011. Since 2013, Iâve been the industry leader for the Exploring Technology module for DMC in Stockholm, getting 80 students to build a co-created interactive art exhibition. 2013-2016 I founded and ran Stockholms first commercial makerspace, Makerspark, a 200sqm store, workshop, co-working and event space in old town. Iâm currently involved in the toy company Strawbees, and Swedenâs largest Bitcoin market, bt.cx.

Burning Man has been my second home since 2009, and worked some with the Burning Man organization both in US and internationally. Iâm one of the community leaders of The Borderland, a co-created, self-managed festival that has now grown to 1800 people outside of Copenhagen (photo attached). I also like helping my wife Malin build awesome interactive optical fibre textile art installations (www.malinbobeck.se).

I grew up on stage and have a talent for speaking (both about things I know, and things I donât know). Iâve done about 70 speaking gigs in the last year, and Iâm nominated for speaker of the year in Sweden. Currently, 90% of my speaking is in Sweden, and I would love to get out more. This is one of the reasons Iâm reconnecting with this group. Letâs go play!

Current favorite book: Hanzi Freinacht - The Listening Society
https://www.amazon.com/Listening-Society-Metamodern-Politics-Guides-ebook/dp/B074MKQ4LR
Current favorite song: Saqi, The Polish Ambassador - Jarro
https://open.spotify.com/track/5tdUXm1qteka7H65addjgG

Finally. Iâve cleared my calendar all of January and will be going to south east Asia (Bali, maybe?). Who wants to meet up?

IMPORTANT ANNOUNCEMENT - Please tag everyone you know and spam the shit out of Facebook. It is time to register for The Borderland.

Welcome to the Radical Inclusion Lotteryâ¢ for The Borderland 2018!!!1!

Brothers and sisters, foxes and rabbits, makers and unicorns - rejoice!
Registration for The Borderland 2018 is now open!
This year, we will be opening up for 2500 lucky members who will get to pay the membership fee of 750 DKK to attend the yearly gathering of our magnificent community. As previous years, we are working from the principle that âEveryone, to the largest possible extent, should have an equal opportunity to attendâ, and we try to achieve this through our Radical Lottery of Inclusionâ¢ in the same fashion as last year.

All the information YOU need to know to get to join the Borderland can be found here:
http://theborderland.se/memberships/

Registration is open now!

Note that EVERY SINGLE PARTICIPANT will have to register their own profile. If you are bringing your friend, family or lover, they too have to have their own profile. The only exception is for kids under age 13.

Stumbled over the sales statistics of The Borderland 2013 (our third year). We limited the event to 300 people and over the course of 5 months, we had 147 registrations. 100 "early bird" for 400 SEK and 47 late for 700 SEK.
153 spots were left unclaimed :'(

Who's a leader anyway?
Apply for The Co-Creative Leadership Development Program!

At The Borderland, everyone is a leader. But where do we find the skill, knowledge and mindset to be those leaders? We have great many initiatives to develop dreams and realities at The Borderland, but how do we develop the people behind them?
Introducing The Borderland Co-Creative Leadership Development Program, a 3 month training for leaders working with co-creative event production. The program will kick of with a retreat in Stockholm on May 3-5 and follow up with online seminars and coaching over the course of the summer. It will cover leadership theory, practical skill training and personal development exercises, and will be hosted by a group of professional facilitators with experience from both The Borderland and the default world.

We are looking for 21 aspiring leaders, who might not have a lot of experience with co-creative event production, but who are dedicated to growth and learning. Two thirds of the spots will be directed for those of you who are intending to step up in leadership at this year's Borderland, be it through infrastructure groups, camp organizing or art projects. One third of the class will be from outside The Borderland community, to provide diversity of views and backgrounds.

Those who finish the program will be awarded an official Co-Creative Leadership Diploma to put in their CVs... Because we can do that.

Does this sound like you? Do you know someone who should apply? 

Application deadline March 17.

Sign up here: https://guff.typeform.com/to/gJIxsd

In 2012, The Borderland was for the first time visited by 3 Scottish Lords, who brought a piano, a stuffed badger and held a Kazoo-infused Scottish parade around our lands.
These men are REAL Lords, having purchased land in Scotland (a square foot each, if I remember correctly), and registered an official Lordship of said land.
Today, we have all started the path to becoming actual Lords and Ladies of The Borderland, reigning over our own private property! With the Borderlandland, we all join the nobility!

All hail the Lords and Ladies of The Borderland!

**ANNOUNCING: The 2021 Borderland Board Lottery!**

Yes, it is true! The Borderland is not only a community where memberships are distributed by lottery - but even leadership is! The nomination committee has once again decided to run a **Board Lottery!**

To apply, you must have attended The Borderland at least once. No other prerequisites! **Sign up and the raffle will decide!**

The winners will be recommended by the nomination committee for the board of GrÃ¤nslandet Ideell FÃ¶rening, for the year of 2022.

Get your lottery ticket NOW! The **deadline is Sun Nov 7 at 23.59.**

Click the link to read more and sign up!
https://guff.typeform.com/to/T4NmbmwX

Check this thread on talk, if you want to understand why lotteries are **the superior** way of choosing organizational leadership. https://talk.theborderland.se/d/iJTt8dUI/electing-the-board-through-lotteri-

This is @[550206780:2048:Daniel] and I, doing one of the very first shifts ever for The Borderland Clown Police, the year it was created: 2013. Noses, batons, helmet, soap bubble gun. Ready for duty.

The Clown Police is the core of our safety work at The Borderland.

The idea of a Clown Police came from the 2002 live role playing game Futuredrome, where the group was tasked by the artificial intelligence controlling the city to "Arrest anyone not having fun enough".

We liked the idea of approaching the issue of safety, not just as mitigation ("let's not have bad things happen"), but as promoting something, of spreading joy! ("let's have good things happen!").

Over 7 years, over 1000 people have "clowned" at The Borderland. I still do it every year, and it's something that really makes me connect with the land and the community. It makes me feel a strong sense of belonging and ownership. I warmly recommend it, especially for newbies, since it's such a fast route into understanding the event and the culture.

There are still plenty of shifts that needs to be taken, so please do a service to yourself and to our community and sign up here

https://coda.io/d/Clown-police-2022_dN7sWwrsLHW/Welcome-to_suUux

Check above link to ***coda **Clown Police 2022* and follow the instructions upon **SIGN UP button**.

In Swedish tradition, dating back at least a millennia, families would plant a "vÃ¥rdtrÃ¤d" in the middle of their yard as they moved to a new property. It would become the home of the "vÃ¥rd" (ward), a spirit that would protect the family from evil, and to serve as a spiritual link across generations.

The most common vÃ¥rdtrÃ¤d was the Oak.

It is said that an Oak will grow for 300 years, live for 300 years, and then die for another 300 years. In cultures across the world it has been a symbol for longevity, stability wisdom, and power. It is a pillar of the Swedish ecosystem, with over 1500 species of fungi, moss, lichen and bugs being more or less dependent on it.

**I want to plant an Oak in AlversjÃ¶ during The Borderland.**

I'm not an arborist or an experienced gardener, so I need all the advice and help I can get. We got to plan logistics, get digging, decorating the space, creating rituals, chanting, blessing, etc. etc.

Of course, we also need people who are decently local, to help with occasional caretaking throughout the year.

â*A society grows great when old men plant trees in whose shade they shall never sit.*â â Greek Proverb.

If you're interested, please join this WhatsApp group: https://chat.whatsapp.com/GiJAEN4hyVHEs4eWKXtvOs

This is the VÃ¥rdtrÃ¤d, the "ward tree", of The Borderland.
We had so many people who helped dig, plant, chant and bless the tree throughout the week, and now it is there. 
Planted, watching over us, for hundreds of years to come.
But, we're still not done. After all, the tree is just a home, a vessel. We need the VÃ¥rd to want to move in and protect us and our lands. For this, the needs constant care, as we send our welcome to the VÃ¥rd.
Some have commented "But that's not an oak!"... Well, it is.
It is a "Flikbladig Skogsek". It is forest oak, Quercus Robur, but a subspecies with finer leaves. It was bought from Spakarps Plantskola, just 10km away from AlversjÃ¶.
It has been taken well care of and is currently in good health. But planting in July is not optimal, and the tree needs a lot of water.
We have a schedule for watering the oak until the fall come. The water bags needs to be filled twice a week, unless it rains.
If you live close to AlversjÃ¶, or if you happen to be in the area, please consider swinging by and giving the Oak som watery love. Pease sign your name if you plan to go, or happened to swing by, so that we can keep track.
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Vgl84GhXCZXmEG3n8OU3qcq0RfTOqQxuU4-u6ziRWlM/edit
Finally, I'd like to thank Peter, Johanna, Nicklas, Poria, Amanda, Nils and Petter, and many more who helped out. Also, a special thanks to Poria, Marie, Caroline, Catrine, Hugo, Camila, Ola, IrÃ©ne, Jacob, Andrew, Viggo and William for their generous donations to the project â¤ï¸

**Letâs bring out the Nordic Warrior!**

**13th MKP Nordic New Warrior Training Adventure**

**April 20th - April 23st 2022 a**t Mundekulla

**Men, New Warriors, Brothers**

On April 20th we will be gathering to initiate a group of men looking for their authentic masculine self. This will be the first move towards creating a **NWTA in a Nordic language**. All initiates are required to understand Swedish, so that some processes, or parts of processes, can be done in Swedish.

These men will come to us with their wounds, their shame and their hidden shadows. Each pair of eyes will be looking for a man they can trust. This will require an extraordinary team of Swedish and international brothers, holding this delicate new space. **Are you willing to join that team?**

I call out to all experienced **Swedish brothers** for support. We donât have many men who have done more than 2-3 trainings, so we really need your experience now.

I call out to all **international brothers** comfortable moving authentically through a multilingual space. Most of the NWTA will be in English, so you will have plenty to do. And as one of our leaders once told me: âAll men cry in the same language.â We need your experience now more than ever.

Itâs an honor for me to be given this opportunity to lead through this important transition, and I humbly ask for your support.

HÃ¶r mig!

Gustaf Tadaa

# A word about ***giving*** advice

A lot has been written about our Advice Process and how to seek advice as you make decisions. However, not so much have been said about it actually means to ***give*** advice in such a process. So here are a few thoughts.
* Advice is a gift. As written in the *gifting* principle, "The value of a gift is unconditional. Gifting does not contemplate a return or an exchange for something of equal value." Your advice is Your gift to the person seeking advice, it is not a tool for you to get what you want.
* The objective of an advice giver is to make the advice seeker as well informed as possible. You are joining in a quest to make them understand the issue better, adding more perspectives and deeper levels of analysis. You want to help make them the best person possible to make the decision.
* Your opinion is not an advice. As you share your view, ask yourself "Does this help the advice seeker understand more about the world, or more about me?".
* Repeating something already said, or chiming in to agree or disagree, is not to provide advice.
* Arguing is not to provide advice.

To seek advice, especially on a contentious topic, is quite a cumbersome task. Our community lives on gifted time, a very scarce and precious resource. As you participate in these processes, please be mindful of how this collective resource is spent.

YEoS-rÃ¤var!
NÃ¤sta Onsdag kÃ¶r vi en exklusiv visning av vÃ¥r konst pÃ¥ det Ã¤nnu oÃ¶ppnade museet NAOM (Fotografiska-Janne Bromans nya stÃ¤lle). Pinga mig om ni vill komma och kika.

ANDRUM
The Borderland is our home. This is the soil where we grew our love, our family, and our art. It is an emotional experience to bring our work back home, to share the work that now sustains our children with the community it grew out of.

ANDRUM was the first rendition of a dream we have been growing for a while. It was a chance for us to test or vision in a space where we feel safe. 

ANDRUM is a visualisation of a deep breath. We recorded @[710302176:2048:Malin]'s breathing as she was meditating, using a mask to measure airflow. This data was the seed for the generative animations that play out through the optical fiber fabrics. The fabric itself was woven from scratch by Malin.

The installation is a continuation of Andetag, a series of 50 artworks that breath in perfect synchronisation no matter where they are placed in the world. ANDRUM was a part of this global breath, and as you were breathing with it at The Borderland, you shared breaths with people in Stockholm, in New York and Long Island.

The final version of ANDRUM will be about 4 times the size and open at a museum in Stockholm next February.

Thank you to @[635779491:2048:Cecilia Pfaff], @[505092055:2048:Charlie Caper], @[529277186:2048:Ludvig von Bahr], @[100000764191454:2048:Viktor Ansund], @[559833950:2048:Andreas HÃ¤mÃ¤lÃ¤inen], and @[514174817:2048:Oliver Vikbladh], who helped us make this happen.

If you have any photos or videos from the space, we would love to see them. It's hard to do it justice, but still nice to have see.

Thank you for breathing with us. ð«ð¨
